criteria.label: Compute Gini impurity and entropy for a small node by hand.
criteria.example: Node with 40 class-0 and 10 class-1: p₀=0.8, p₁=0.2 → Gini = 1 − (0.64 + 0.04) = 0.32; entropy ≈ −(0.8 log₂ 0.8 + 0.2 log₂ 0.2) ≈ 0.72 bits.

criteria.label: Explain information gain for a candidate split and why CART is greedy.
criteria.example: Parent entropy 0.72 → split into left (entropy 0.1) and right (entropy 0.5) weighted by child sizes → gain = 0.72 − weighted average. Greedy: pick best split now without revisiting earlier nodes.

criteria.label: Train a DecisionTreeClassifier, tune max_depth with cross-validation, and interpret export_text rules.
criteria.example: Iris: CV max_depth in [1,3,5,7]; best depth=3; read rule `petal length (cm) <= 2.45` as the root split separating Setosa.

criteria.label: Contrast classification vs regression tree impurity and leaf predictions.
criteria.example: Classifier: Gini/entropy + majority vote. Regressor: variance reduction + mean y. Same recursive split search, different I(·) and leaf value.

criteria.label: Diagnose overfitting from train vs validation curves over max_depth and recommend constraints.
criteria.example: Train acc → 1.0 by depth 12 while val acc peaks at depth 5 then drops — set max_depth=5, min_samples_leaf=10, or use cost-complexity pruning.
